note_data.title: Introduction to organic chemistry
keyPoints: **Organic compounds** contain carbon atoms covalently bonded to hydrogen and other elements, forming the basis of all living matter
**Homologous series** are families of organic compounds with the same functional group and general formula, differing by CH₂ units
**Structural isomers** have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements of atoms
**Functional groups** are specific arrangements of atoms that determine the chemical properties of organic molecules
**IUPAC nomenclature** follows systematic rules: identify longest carbon chain, number from end nearest functional group, name substituents alphabetically
**Empirical formula** shows the simplest whole number ratio of atoms, while **molecular formula** shows actual number of each type of atom
**Displayed formula** shows all covalent bonds and atoms, **structural formula** shows arrangement without showing all bonds
**Chain isomers** differ in carbon skeleton arrangement, **positional isomers** have functional groups in different positions
**Primary carbons** are bonded to one other carbon, **secondary** to two carbons, **tertiary** to three carbons

definitions.definition: A family of organic compounds with the same functional group and general formula, where successive members differ by CH₂
Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements of atoms
An atom or group of atoms responsible for the characteristic chemical reactions of a compound
The simplest whole number ratio of atoms of each element present in a compound
The simplest algebraic formula for any member of a homologous series

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the name or formula directly. No explanation needed.
Give the reason why using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show all atoms and bonds clearly. Label if asked.
Use IUPAC rules: longest chain, number from functional group end, alphabetical substituents

modelAnswer.question: Butanol has the molecular formula C₄H₁₀O. Draw the displayed formulae of two structural isomers of butanol and name them using IUPAC nomenclature. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: First isomer: butan-1-ol - H₃C-CH₂-CH₂-CH₂OH (displayed formula showing all C-H and C-C bonds). Second isomer: butan-2-ol - H₃C-CH₂-CH(OH)-CH₃ (displayed formula showing all bonds). Both formulae must show all atoms and all covalent bonds clearly.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct displayed formula for butan-1-ol
Mark 3: Correct displayed formula for butan-2-ol

commonMistakes: Never write 'CH₃CH₂CH₂CH₂OH' for displayed formula — must show all individual bonds and atoms
Students often number carbon chain from wrong end — always start from end nearest functional group
Writing 'butyl alcohol' instead of IUPAC name 'butan-1-ol' — use systematic naming
Confusing empirical and molecular formulae — empirical is simplest ratio, molecular is actual numbers
Missing the -e when naming alkanes with functional groups (e.g. 'butan-1-ol' not 'butane-1-ol')
examinerTips: Nomenclature questions appear in every Paper 1 and Paper 2 — learn IUPAC rules thoroughly
Always count carbons carefully when drawing isomers — easy marks lost through careless errors
For displayed formulae, show every single bond — condensed formulae lose marks
Check molecular formulae match when drawing isomers — must have same number of each atom
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Displayed formulae showing all atoms and covalent bonds for structural isomers, with clear labeling of functional groups and carbon chain numbering for IUPAC nomenclature
